title: Tool Choice, Evidence, Actions, and Approval Boundaries · Failure Case: Tool Description
summary: Design a tool-using investigation that reads evidence before changing state. Planned artifact: Add and verify the prompt tool description increment in a safe tool-use decision protocol. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Write tool descriptions and schemas that make capability, inputs, outputs, and side effects unambiguous.
Require bounded uncertainty, missing evidence, and blockers to remain visible.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.prompt:
# Cross-agent prompt portfolio
# Add tested contracts below. Keep shared intent separate from Claude and Codex adaptations.

steps.title: Predict Tool Description
steps.instruction:
Use a changed theory case derived from this module: prompt tools actions. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed theory case derived from this module: prompt tools actions. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. Before reading, predict which action preserves the Tool Description contract.

content.text: Write tool descriptions and schemas that make capability, inputs, outputs, and side effects unambiguous. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move

content.title: Worked case pattern

content.title: Non-worked case

steps.title: Build Tool Description evidence
steps.instruction: Append a contract headed "Evidence: prompt-tool-description-ciyqdt" with concrete Goal, Context, Constraints, Output, and Done when sections tailored to this case.

steps.title: Defend the Tool Description model

steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
